# UC-22.1.13 · GDPR High-Risk Breach Communication to Data Subjects

## Description

Article 34 requires controllers to communicate personal data breaches directly to affected data subjects "without undue delay" when the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms. While Art. 33 covers DPA notification, Art. 34 addresses the often-overlooked obligation to notify individuals. This use case tracks whether high-risk breaches have triggered individual notification workflows and monitors their completion.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Create `gdpr_breach_notifications.csv` KV store linking notable IDs to notification status; (2) when a breach involves special category data, financial data, or affects >1000 subjects, default `art34_required` to "yes"; (3) alert the DPO on pending Art. 34 notifications; (4) track notification method (email, letter, public notice) and completion dates; (5) document Art. 34(3) exceptions (encrypted data, mitigation applied, disproportionate effort requiring public communication). Indexes required: `index=itsm`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (notification workflow tickets), `gdpr_breach_notifications.csv` (KV store).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `gdpr_breach_notifications.csv` KV store linking notable IDs to notification status; (2) when a breach involves special category data, financial data, or affects >1000 subjects, default `art34_required` to "yes"; (3) alert the DPO on pending Art. 34 notifications; (4) track notification method (email, letter, public notice) and completion dates; (5) document Art. 34(3) exceptions (encrypted data, mitigation applied, disproportionate effort requiring public communication).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency="critical" earliest=-30d
| lookup gdpr_breach_notifications.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT art33_notified, art34_required, art34_notified, art34_date, subjects_notified_count
| eval art34_status=case(
    art34_required="yes" AND art34_notified="yes", "COMPLETE",
    art34_required="yes" AND isnull(art34_notified), "PENDING — notify subjects",
    art34_required="no", "Not required",
    isnull(art34_required), "ASSESS — determine if Art.34 applies",
    1=1, "Unknown")
| where art34_status IN ("PENDING — notify subjects", "ASSESS — determine if Art.34 applies")
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, owner, art33_notified, art34_required, art34_status
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR High-Risk Breach Communication to Data Subjects (Art. 34)** — Article 34 requires controllers to communicate personal data breaches directly to affected data subjects "without undue delay" when the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms. While Art. 33 covers DPA notification, Art. 34 addresses the often-overlooked obligation to notify individuals. This use case tracks whether high-risk breaches have triggered individual notification workflows and monitors their completion.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (notification workflow tickets), `gdpr_breach_notifications.csv` (KV store). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **art34_status** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where art34_status IN ("PENDING — notify subjects", "ASSESS — determine if Art.34 applies")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: _time, art33_notified, art34_required, art34_status, owner, rule_name, urgency.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample results with the ES Incident Review queue and analyst outcomes so counts, severities, and fields match what your security operations team triages in production.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (pending individual notifications), Single value (breaches requiring Art. 34 notification), Bar chart (notification status distribution), Timeline (notification lifecycle).

## SPL

```spl
`notable` urgency="critical" earliest=-30d
| lookup gdpr_breach_notifications.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT art33_notified, art34_required, art34_notified, art34_date, subjects_notified_count
| eval art34_status=case(
    art34_required="yes" AND art34_notified="yes", "COMPLETE",
    art34_required="yes" AND isnull(art34_notified), "PENDING — notify subjects",
    art34_required="no", "Not required",
    isnull(art34_required), "ASSESS — determine if Art.34 applies",
    1=1, "Unknown")
| where art34_status IN ("PENDING — notify subjects", "ASSESS — determine if Art.34 applies")
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, owner, art33_notified, art34_required, art34_status
| sort - _time
```

## Visualization

Table (pending individual notifications), Single value (breaches requiring Art. 34 notification), Bar chart (notification status distribution), Timeline (notification lifecycle). Key fields: `art33_notified`, `art34_required`, `art34_status`, `owner`, `rule_name`, `urgency`.
